name: debugging
tagline: Hypothesis-driven debugging index skill that routes an agent to runtime/tool/phase reference files for cross-language crash and failure diag
verdict: No security red flags found: no credential reads, no network egress, no shell-piped remote code, no destructive ops, nothing hidden. It even bans `git commit` from inside the skill and forbids silently swallowing errors. Minor rough edges: no explicit negative-trigger guidance, no version/changelog signal in frontmatter, and no worked example of a full phase

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do NOT use when...' guidance anywhere in frontmatter or body — a user can't easily tell when this heavyweight process is overkill (e.g. a one-line typo fix).
No single explicit statement of what final output/deliverable should look like beyond 'fix + test' implied in Phase 9/10 cleanup — reasonably inferable but not spelled out as a contract.
No worked example or sample transcript of a full hypothesis-formation or Oracle Triple cycle; relies entirely on referenced files (not included here) for concreteness.
